---
title: "Command Architecture Roadmap"
---

> **See also:** [Inter-Widget Communication Roadmap — Ownership & Registration Best Practices](../../inter_widget_communication_roadmap.md#ownership--registration-best-practices) for guidance on where Commands vs ContextActions should be defined as operations scale.

## Progress Summary

*Last updated: 2026-03-12*

| Phase | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Phase 1:** Command Foundation | **Complete** | 6 commands, factory, validation, variable substitution, recorder, mutation guard, golden traces |
| **Phase 2:** Triage Session | **Nearly Complete** | Core, widget, guided editor all done. Keyboard shortcuts (2.4) and tracked-regions overlay (2.5) remain. |
| **Phase 3:** Persistence & Save Registry | **Complete** | SaverRegistry, 8 savers (CSV ×6, CapnProto, OpenCV), SaveData + LoadData commands, round-trip fuzz tests |
| **Phase 3.5:** Command Recording & Test Generation | Not Started | Wire CommandRecorder into GUI, route widgets through executeSequence(), minimal Command Log UI |
| **Phase 4:** Action Journal Integration | Not Started | Generalizes CommandRecorder into persistent, timestamped journal |
| **Phase 5:** Undo/Redo | Not Started | CommandStack, memento-based EditorState undo, inverse commands |
| **Phase 6:** CLI Batch & Macro Recording | Not Started | Headless command execution; generalizes golden trace tests |
| **Phase 7:** Command Registration API | Not Started | Replace hardcoded if-else factory with explicit-dispatcher registration; enable domain libraries to contribute commands |
| **Widget Command-ification** | Ongoing | 9 new commands identified via audit; none yet implemented. See [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd). |

For the full architectural design, types, and code examples, see the
[Command Architecture Design Document](index.qmd).

## What Exists Today

### Registered Commands (6)

| Command | Category | Undoable | Description |
|---------|----------|----------|-------------|
| `MoveByTimeRange` | data_mutation | Yes | Move entities in a frame range between same-type data objects |
| `CopyByTimeRange` | data_mutation | No | Copy entities (source unmodified) |
| `AddInterval` | data_mutation | No | Append interval to `DigitalIntervalSeries`; creates series if missing |
| `ForEachKey` | meta | No | Iterate a list of values, execute sub-commands per item |
| `SaveData` | persistence | No | Save a data object via `LoaderRegistry::trySave()` |
| `LoadData` | persistence | Yes | Load a data object via `LoaderRegistry::tryLoad()` |

### Infrastructure

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `CommandFactory` | `src/Commands/Core/` | `createCommand()`, `createCommandFromJson()`, `getAvailableCommands()`, `getCommandInfo()` |
| `CommandRecorder` | `src/Commands/Core/` | Records executed commands for replay; hook in `executeSequence()` |
| `MutationGuard` | `src/DataManager/Commands/` | Debug-only: warns when `DataManager` is mutated outside a command |
| `DataManagerSnapshot` | `src/DataManager/` | Lightweight state snapshot (type, entity count, content hash) for golden trace tests |
| `ParameterSchema` | `src/ParameterSchema/` | Compile-time parameter schema extraction from reflect-cpp structs |
| `AutoParamWidget` | `src/WhiskerToolbox/AutoParamWidget/` | Dynamic Qt form builder from `ParameterSchema` |
| `TriageSession` | `src/TriageSession/` | Mark/Commit/Recall state machine using command sequences |
| `SaverRegistry` | `src/DataManager/IO/core/` | `SaverInfo`, `AtomicWrite`, `getSupportedSaveFormats()` |
| `Validation` | `src/Commands/Core/` | `validateSequence()` — structural, type, and variable checks |
| `VariableSubstitution` | `src/Commands/Core/` | Two-pass `${variable}` substitution + `normalizeJsonNumbers()` |

### Test Coverage

- **Command unit tests**: 25+ test cases (factory, move, copy, interval, forEach, error handling)
- **CommandRecorder tests**: 19 test cases (record/clear, JSON serialization, executeSequence integration, MutationGuard)
- **Validation tests**: 10+ test cases (structural, deserialization, variable resolution, type compatibility)
- **Variable substitution tests**: 10+ test cases (static/runtime variables, integer normalization)
- **SaveData/LoadData tests**: 22 test cases (factory, execution, undo, serialization, round-trip)
- **Saver fuzz tests**: 9 fuzz test files (CSV round-trip for all 6 types, CapnProto lines, OpenCV masks)
- **TriageSession tests**: State machine transitions, runtime variable population, integration
- **Golden trace tests**: CopyByTimeRange, MoveByTimeRange, AddInterval, multi-command sequences, replay determinism

## Dependency Graph (Remaining Work)

```
Completed Foundation (Phases 1, 3)
   │
   ├──► Phase 2 Remaining: Keyboard shortcuts (2.4), tracked-regions viz (2.5)
   │
   ├──► Phase 3.5: Command Recording & Test Generation
   │       Wires CommandRecorder into GUI, routes widgets through executeSequence()
   │       Enables: "do it in GUI → export JSON → write golden trace test"
   │
   ├──► Widget Command-ification (ongoing, on-demand)
   │       Uses: MutationGuard for gap detection, golden traces for verification
   │       Blocked commands: CreateData, DeleteData, AddEvent, RemoveEvent, etc.
   │
   ├──► Phase 4: Action Journal
   │       Depends on: Phase 3.5 (recorder wired into app)
   │       Generalizes recorder into persistent timestamped log
   │
   ├──► Phase 5: Undo/Redo
   │       Independent of Phases 2, 4, 6
   │       MoveByTimeRange already supports undo; needs CommandStack
   │
   ├──► Phase 6: CLI Batch & Macro Recording
   │       Depends on: Phases 3 (done), 4
   │       Generalizes golden trace tests into CLI tool
   │
   └──► Phase 7: Command Registration API (Explicit Dispatcher)
           Independent of all other phases
           Replaces hardcoded if-else factory with explicit registration functions
           Enables: domain libraries (TransformsV2, MLCore) to contribute commands
           No --whole-archive required; linker errors on misconfiguration
```

**Parallelizable:** Phases 3.5, 5, 7, and widget command-ification can proceed independently.
Phase 4 depends on Phase 3.5. Phase 6 requires Phase 4.

---

## Completed Phases (Summary)

### Phase 1 — Command Foundation (Complete)

All 14 steps complete. Delivered:

- **Core types** (Steps 1.1–1.3): `CommandResult`, `CommandContext`, `CommandDescriptor`,
  `CommandSequenceDescriptor`, `ICommand` base class, `CommandFactory` with
  `createCommand()` / `createCommandFromJson()`.
- **Execution engine** (Steps 1.4–1.5): Two-pass `${variable}` substitution,
  `executeSequence()` with stop-on-error, `SequenceResult` for undo.
- **Query helpers** (Step 1.6): `getEntityIdsInRange()` for `RaggedTimeSeries<T>`,
  `DigitalEventSeries`, and `DigitalIntervalSeries`.
- **First commands** (Step 1.7): `MoveByTimeRange`, `CopyByTimeRange`, `AddInterval`,
  `ForEachKey`.
- **Validation** (Step 1.9): `validateSequence()` with structural, deserialization,
  variable, key existence, and type compatibility checks.
- **GUI verb audit** (Step 1.10): On-demand hybrid strategy. DataManager_Widget and
  DataInspector_Widget audited. See [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd).
- **ParameterSchema** (Step 1.11): Extracted to `src/ParameterSchema/` as standalone library.
- **CommandInfo** (Step 1.12): `getAvailableCommands()`, `getCommandInfo()`, per-command
  `ParameterUIHints`.
- **CommandRecorder + MutationGuard** (Step 1.13): Trace recording with `executeSequence()`
  hook; debug-only strangulation pattern for runtime gap detection.
- **DataManagerSnapshot + Golden Traces** (Step 1.14): Lightweight snapshot comparison;
  golden trace test pattern for widget verification.

**Known pitfall (resolved):** `rfl::Generic` stores integers as `double`, causing round-trip
failures. Fixed via `normalizeJsonNumbers()` in `VariableSubstitution` and
`createCommandFromJson()` in `CommandFactory`.

See the [design document](index.qmd) for architecture details.

### Phase 3 — Persistence Commands & Save Registry (Complete)

All steps complete. Delivered:

- **SaverRegistry infrastructure** (Step 3.1.1): `SaverInfo` metadata, `AtomicWrite` utility,
  `getSupportedSaveFormats()` on `LoaderRegistry`.
  See [SaverInfo & AtomicWrite](../../IO/saver_infrastructure.qmd).
- **8 format savers** (Steps 3.1.2–3.1.9):

  | Format | Data Type | Fuzz Tested |
  |--------|-----------|-------------|
  | CSV | PointData | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CSV | LineData (single-file) | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CSV | LineData (multi-file) | Yes (round-trip) |
  | CSV | AnalogTimeSeries | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CSV | DigitalEventSeries | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CSV | DigitalIntervalSeries | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CSV | MaskData (RLE) | Yes (round-trip + delimiter) |
  | CapnProto | LineData | Yes (exact binary round-trip) |
  | OpenCV (Image) | MaskData | Yes (robustness) |

- **SaveData command** (Step 3.2): Dispatches via `LoaderRegistry::trySave()`. Not undoable.
  See [SaveData Command](save_data.qmd).
- **LoadData command** (Step 3.3): Dispatches via `LoaderRegistry::tryLoad()`. Undoable
  (undo calls `deleteData()`). See [LoadData Command](load_data.qmd).

---

## Phase 2 — Triage Session (Nearly Complete)

**Goal:** Mark/Commit/Recall workflow using command sequences.

**Completed steps:**

- **TriageSession core** (Step 2.1): `Idle ↔ Marking` state machine in `src/TriageSession/`.
  `commit()` populates runtime variables (`mark_frame`, `current_frame`) and delegates to
  `executeSequence()`. Retains last commit commands for undo support.
- **TriageSessionState** (Step 2.2): EditorState subclass with pipeline/tracked-regions
  serialization.
- **TriageSessionWidget** (Step 2.3): Mark/Commit/Recall buttons, pipeline display,
  tracked-regions summary.
- **Unit tests** (Step 2.6): State machine transitions, runtime variable population,
  full triage round-trip integration test.
- **AutoParamWidget extraction** (Step 2.7): Extracted to `src/WhiskerToolbox/AutoParamWidget/`
  with `Qt6::Widgets` + `ParameterSchema` dependencies only.
- **Guided Pipeline Editor** (Step 2.8): Command picker dialog, editable command list with
  `AutoParamWidget` rows, bidirectional sync with raw JSON editor.

### Remaining Steps

#### Step 2.4 — Keyboard Shortcuts

- [ ] **M** — Mark (start a triage range)
- [ ] **C** — Commit (execute pipeline for `[mark_frame, current_frame]`)
- [ ] **R** — Recall (abort, jump back to mark frame)

#### Step 2.5 — Tracked-Regions Visualization

- [ ] Render tracked-regions `DigitalIntervalSeries` as shaded overlay on the timeline/scrubber

---

## Widget Command-ification (Ongoing)

**Goal:** Route all `DataManager` mutations through commands. Audited on-demand per widget.

**Strategy:** See [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd) for the full audit process, widget
tables, and command designs.

### Definition of Done

A widget is fully command-ified when:

1. **Zero direct `DataManager` mutations** — all `setData()`, `deleteData()`, etc. go through
   `createCommand()` + `execute()`.
2. **Zero MutationGuard warnings** — exercising the widget in a debug build produces no
   console warnings.
3. **Golden trace test exists** — at least one Catch2 test replays a recorded command
   sequence and asserts the final `DataManagerSnapshot`.

### Audited Widgets

| Widget | Verbs | Covered | New Cmds Needed | Golden Trace |
|--------|-------|---------|-----------------|--------------|
| DataManager_Widget | 3 | 0 | 2 (`CreateData`, `DeleteData`) | Not yet |
| DataInspector_Widget | ~30 | ~10 | ~14 | Not yet |

### Commands Still Needed

9 commands identified by audit but not yet implemented. See
[New Commands Identified](gui_verb_audit.qmd#new-commands-identified) for full designs.

**Wave 1 — Core CRUD:** `CreateData`, `DeleteData`, `AddEvent`, `RemoveEvent`

**Wave 2 — Entity Operations:** `DeleteByEntityIds`, `RemoveInterval`, `ModifyInterval`

**Wave 3 — Group Operations:** `AssignToGroup`, `RemoveFromGroup`

Remaining widgets (DataImport, DataExport, Media, etc.) are audited on-demand as they are
touched. MutationGuard provides automated runtime gap detection.

---

## Phase 3.5 — Command Recording & Test Generation

**Goal:** Wire `CommandRecorder` into the running application so GUI actions produce
exportable `CommandSequenceDescriptor` JSON, enabling semi-automated golden trace test
generation.

**Depends on:** Phase 1 (done)

**Motivation:** All the infrastructure exists (`CommandRecorder`, `executeSequence()` hook,
`DataManagerSnapshot`, golden trace tests) but nothing is connected in the running app.
Widgets call `cmd.execute(ctx)` directly, bypassing `executeSequence()` and its recording
hook. This phase wires everything together so a user can: *do actions in GUI → see recorded
commands → copy JSON → paste into a Catch2 golden trace test*.

### Current Gap

| Component | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| `CommandRecorder` class | ✅ Implemented, tested (19 test cases) |
| `executeSequence()` recording hook | ✅ Implemented (accepts `CommandRecorder*` param) |
| `MutationGuard` | ✅ Active in debug builds |
| Shared recorder instance in app | ✅ `MainWindow` owns `CommandRecorder`; `commandRecorder()` getter |
| `ctx.recorder` fallback | ✅ `executeSequence()` falls back to `ctx.recorder` when no explicit recorder passed |
| DataSynthesizer "Generate" | ✅ Routes through `executeSequence()` |
| SaveData call sites | ✅ Routes through `executeSequence()` |
| TriageSession `commit()` | ✅ Routes through `executeSequence()` with recorder |
| UI to view/export recorded commands | ✅ CommandLog_Widget (Step 3.5.3) |

### Step 3.5.1 — Centralized Recorder (Complete)

- [x] Add `CommandRecorder*` field to `CommandContext` (`src/Commands/Core/CommandContext.hpp`).
  Backwards-compatible default of `nullptr`.
- [x] Update `executeSequence()` (`src/Commands/Core/SequenceExecution.cpp`) to use
  `ctx.recorder` as fallback when the explicit `recorder` parameter is `nullptr`.
- [x] Add `std::unique_ptr<commands::CommandRecorder>` to `MainWindow`
  (`src/WhiskerToolbox/Main_Window/mainwindow.hpp`). Expose via `commandRecorder()` getter.
  Populate `CommandContext::recorder` for all widget command execution.

### Step 3.5.2 — Route Widgets Through executeSequence()

- [x] **DataSynthesizer Generate** (`src/WhiskerToolbox/DataSynthesizer_Widget/UI/DataSynthesizerProperties_Widget.cpp`):
  Build a `CommandDescriptor{.command_name = "SynthesizeData", .parameters = ...}` and call
  `executeSequence()` instead of `cmd.execute(ctx)`.
- [x] **SaveData call sites** (DataInspector savers, DataExport): Same pattern. See
  [widget_save_refactoring_guide.qmd](widget_save_refactoring_guide.qmd).
- [x] **TriageSession** (`src/TriageSession/TriageSession.cpp`): Accept `CommandRecorder*`
  in `commit()`, forward to `executeSequence()`.

### Step 3.5.3 — Command Log UI (Complete)

- [x] Create a minimal "Command Log" dock widget showing the list of recorded
  `CommandDescriptor` names and parameters from the shared recorder.
  See [Command Log Widget](command_log_widget.qmd).
- [x] "Copy as JSON" button: calls `recorder->toSequence("recorded_session")`, serializes
  via `rfl::json::write()`, copies to clipboard.
- [x] "Clear" button.
- [x] Add "Modules → Command Log" menu item in MainWindow.

**Note:** This is intentionally minimal — NOT the full Phase 4 Action Journal. No
persistence, timestamps, filtering, or provenance. Just enough to see what happened and
export it.

### Step 3.5.4 — End-to-End Verification

- [ ] Demo workflow: Open app → open Command Log → generate a SineWave via Data Synthesizer
  → save as CSV via DataInspector → Command Log shows 2 entries (`SynthesizeData`, `SaveData`)
  → "Copy as JSON" → paste into a Catch2 golden trace test with `DataManagerSnapshot`
  assertions → test passes.
- [ ] Debug build: exercising recorded workflows produces no `MutationGuard` warnings.
- [ ] Round-trip: `executeSequence(recorded_json)` then `executeSequence(recorder.toSequence())`
  produce identical `DataManagerSnapshot`s.

### Exit Criteria

1. All GUI command execution paths go through `executeSequence()` with a recorder.
2. Command Log widget displays recorded commands in real-time.
3. "Copy as JSON" produces a valid `CommandSequenceDescriptor` that, when pasted into a
   Catch2 test and executed via `executeSequence()`, reproduces the same
   `DataManagerSnapshot`.

---

## Phase 4 — Action Journal Integration

**Goal:** Commands auto-record to journal; journal can replay command entries.

**Depends on:** Phase 3.5 (recorder wired into app)

Generalizes the `CommandRecorder` into a persistent, timestamped, filterable log with
workspace integration. The `executeSequence()` hook from Step 1.13 is reused — the change
is swapping `CommandRecorder*` for `ActionJournal*` with persistence and metadata.

### Step 4.1 — Journal Hook in executeSequence

- [ ] Generalize the `CommandRecorder*` parameter into `ActionJournal*`
- [ ] On successful command execution, record `ActionEntry` with timestamp, command
  name/description, serialized parameters, source widget

### Step 4.2 — Journal Replay

- [ ] Deserialize `ActionEntry::payload_json` via `createCommand()` + `execute()` for replay
- [ ] Journal export: filter out undone entries, produce clean `CommandSequenceDescriptor` JSON

### Step 4.3 — WorkspaceManager Integration

- [ ] Connect journal recording to existing `WorkspaceManager` provenance tracking

---

## Phase 5 — Undo/Redo

**Goal:** CommandStack, EditorState memento undo, inverse-command undo for data mutations.

**Depends on:** Phase 1 (done)

### Step 5.1 — CommandStack

- [ ] `CommandStack` class: push executed commands, undo/redo with `CommandContext`
- [ ] Clean state tracking: `markClean()` / `isClean()` for save-state awareness
- [ ] Redo stack cleared on new command push

See [CommandStack](index.qmd#commandstack) in the design document.

### Step 5.2 — WidgetStateChangeCommand

- [ ] Generic memento-based command for any `EditorState`
- [ ] Captures before/after JSON snapshots
- [ ] `mergeWith()` for coalescing rapid changes to the same widget

See [WidgetStateChangeCommand](index.qmd#widgetstatechangecommand) in the design document.

### Step 5.3 — CommandSequenceCommand

- [ ] Wraps `vector<unique_ptr<ICommand>>` for macro undo (e.g., triage commits)
- [ ] `isUndoable()` returns true if any child is undoable
- [ ] `undo()` reverses children in reverse order, skipping non-undoable ones

See [CommandSequenceCommand](index.qmd#commandsequencecommand-triage-undo) in the design document.

### Step 5.4 — MoveByTimeRange Undo Verification

- [ ] Integration test: move → undo → verify original state restored
  (`MoveByTimeRange` already reports `isUndoable() = true`)

### Step 5.5 — UI Integration

- [ ] Edit → Undo / Redo menu items
- [ ] Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Integration with dirty tracking (`EditorState::isDirty()`, `WorkspaceManager`)

---

## Phase 6 — CLI Batch Executor & Macro Recording

**Goal:** Execute command sequences headlessly; record GUI actions as commands.

**Depends on:** Phases 3 (done), 4

Generalizes the golden trace test pattern (load JSON → create `DataManager` →
`executeSequence()` → check results) into a CLI tool. The macro recorder generalizes
`CommandRecorder` from a test utility into a user-facing feature.

### Step 6.1 — CLI Executor

- [ ] CLI tool or application mode: load a `.json` command sequence, create `DataManager`, execute, exit
- [ ] Variable substitution for batch parameterization (reuses existing pipeline variable system)
- [ ] Exit code reflects success/failure

### Step 6.2 — Macro Recorder

- [ ] Intercept GUI actions (data loads, transforms, triage commits) and emit `CommandDescriptor`s
- [ ] Record to an in-memory `CommandSequenceDescriptor` that can be saved to JSON

### Step 6.3 — ConfigExportToolbar

- [ ] Reusable toolbar component: "Copy as JSON", "Save Configuration..."
- [ ] Attach to widgets that produce command sequences (triage session, transform widget, etc.)

### Step 6.4 — CommandSequenceWidget

- [ ] Standalone Qt widget (`src/WhiskerToolbox/Commands/`) for building, editing, and running
  command sequences outside the triage workflow
- [ ] JSON editor with validation feedback
- [ ] Command list view with run button

---

## Phase 7 — Command Registration API (Explicit Dispatcher)

**Goal:** Replace the hardcoded if-else chain in `CommandFactory` with an explicit-dispatcher
registration pattern, enabling domain libraries to contribute commands without `--whole-archive`
linker flags or silent registration failures.

**Depends on:** Phase 1 (done). Independent of all other phases.

**Motivation:** Today, `CommandFactory::createCommandFromJson()` is a 7-branch if-else chain,
and `getAvailableCommands()` returns a hardcoded `std::vector<CommandInfo>`. Adding a command
requires editing `CommandFactory.cpp`, adding `#include` directives for the new command header,
and manually appending to both the creation chain and the info vector. This is fine for the
current ~7 commands (and the ~9 CRUD commands from the [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd)),
but it doesn't scale when domain libraries like TransformsV2 and MLCore need to contribute
commands without creating circular dependencies.

See [Inter-Widget Communication Roadmap — Ownership & Registration Best Practices](../../inter_widget_communication_roadmap.md#ownership--registration-best-practices)
for the full motivation and command ownership tiers.

### Why Explicit Dispatcher Over RAII

The project's existing registries (TransformsV2 `RegisterTransform<>`, DataSynthesizer
`RegisterGenerator<>`) use RAII static registration with `--whole-archive` linking. While
this works, it has significant drawbacks that compound as libraries scale:

| Concern | RAII + `--whole-archive` | Explicit Dispatcher |
|---------|------------------------|---------------------|
| **Failure mode** | **Silent** — missing `--whole-archive` → registrations vanish, no error | **Loud** — missing call → linker error (undefined reference) |
| **Central file touch** | Build file (`CMakeLists.txt`) per new library | Source file (dispatcher `.cpp`) per new library |
| **Binary size** | Forces linker to include every `.o` in the archive | Normal dead-code elimination applies |
| **Platform boilerplate** | 3 platform-specific linker flag variants (Linux/macOS/MSVC) | None |
| **Duplicated across consumers** | Every exe/test must repeat `--whole-archive` flags | Each test calls only the registration functions it needs |
| **Shared library compatibility** | Singleton identity breaks across `.so` boundaries | Works with both static and shared libraries |

The explicit dispatcher follows the Blender operator registration pattern: each module
defines a `register_*_commands()` function, and a central dispatcher in the composition root
calls them all. One line per module, loud failures, no linker magic.

### Current State

```cpp
// CommandFactory.cpp — today
bool isKnownCommandName(std::string const & name) {
    return name == "MoveByTimeRange" || name == "CopyByTimeRange" ||
           name == "AddInterval" || name == "ForEachKey" ||
           name == "SaveData" || name == "LoadData" ||
           name == "SynthesizeData";
}

std::unique_ptr<ICommand> createCommandFromJson(
        std::string const & name, std::string const & params_json) {
    auto const json = normalizeJsonNumbers(params_json);
    if (name == "MoveByTimeRange") { /* deserialize + construct */ }
    if (name == "CopyByTimeRange") { /* ... */ }
    // ... 5 more branches
    return nullptr;
}
```

### Design

#### Step 7.1 — CommandRegistry Singleton

Replace the free functions in `CommandFactory` with a `CommandRegistry` singleton that stores
a `std::unordered_map` from command name to a creator function + metadata:

```cpp
// src/Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.hpp
namespace commands {

/// Type-erased command creator: takes normalized JSON, returns ICommand.
using CommandCreator = std::function<std::unique_ptr<ICommand>(std::string const &)>;

class CommandRegistry {
public:
    static CommandRegistry & instance();

    /// Register a command with its creator and metadata.
    /// @pre name must not already be registered (asserts in debug).
    void registerCommand(std::string name, CommandCreator creator, CommandInfo info);

    /// Query
    [[nodiscard]] bool isKnown(std::string const & name) const;
    [[nodiscard]] std::unique_ptr<ICommand> create(std::string const & name,
                                                    std::string const & params_json) const;
    [[nodiscard]] std::vector<CommandInfo> availableCommands() const;
    [[nodiscard]] std::optional<CommandInfo> commandInfo(std::string const & name) const;

private:
    struct Entry {
        CommandCreator creator;
        CommandInfo info;
    };
    std::unordered_map<std::string, Entry> _commands;
};

} // namespace commands
```

The existing free functions (`isKnownCommandName`, `createCommand`, `createCommandFromJson`,
`getAvailableCommands`, `getCommandInfo`) become thin wrappers that delegate to
`CommandRegistry::instance()`. This preserves backward compatibility — all existing callers
continue to work unchanged.

#### Step 7.2 — Per-Library Registration Functions

Instead of RAII static objects (`RegisterCommand<>` in anonymous namespaces), each library
defines an **explicit registration function** that imperatively registers its commands:

```cpp
// src/Commands/Core/register_core_commands.hpp
namespace commands {
/// Register all built-in commands (MoveByTimeRange, CopyByTimeRange, etc.)
void register_core_commands();
} // namespace commands

// src/Commands/Core/register_core_commands.cpp
#include "CommandRegistry.hpp"
#include "commands/MoveByTimeRange.hpp"
#include "commands/CopyByTimeRange.hpp"
#include "commands/AddInterval.hpp"
#include "commands/ForEachKey.hpp"
#include "commands/SaveData.hpp"
#include "commands/LoadData.hpp"
#include "commands/SynthesizeData.hpp"

namespace commands {

void register_core_commands() {
    auto & reg = CommandRegistry::instance();

    reg.registerCommand(
        "MoveByTimeRange",
        [](std::string const & json) -> std::unique_ptr<ICommand> {
            auto p = rfl::json::read<MoveByTimeRangeParams>(normalizeJsonNumbers(json));
            if (!p) return nullptr;
            return std::make_unique<MoveByTimeRange>(std::move(*p));
        },
        CommandInfo{
            .name = "MoveByTimeRange",
            .description = "Move entities in a time range between same-type data objects",
            .category = "data_mutation",
            .supports_undo = true,
            .supported_data_types = {"LineData", "PointData", "MaskData", "DigitalEventSeries"},
            .parameter_schema = extractParameterSchema<MoveByTimeRangeParams>(),
        });

    // ... repeat for each built-in command
}

} // namespace commands
```

A convenience helper template can reduce the boilerplate:

```cpp
// src/Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.hpp
namespace commands {

/// Helper: register a command with automatic JSON deserialization and schema extraction.
template<typename CommandT, typename Params>
void registerTypedCommand(CommandRegistry & reg, std::string name, CommandInfo info) {
    info.parameter_schema = extractParameterSchema<Params>();
    reg.registerCommand(
        std::move(name),
        [](std::string const & params_json) -> std::unique_ptr<ICommand> {
            auto const json = normalizeJsonNumbers(params_json);
            auto p = rfl::json::read<Params>(json);
            if (!p) return nullptr;
            return std::make_unique<CommandT>(std::move(*p));
        },
        std::move(info));
}

} // namespace commands
```

With this helper, each registration is a single call:

```cpp
void register_core_commands() {
    auto & reg = CommandRegistry::instance();
    registerTypedCommand<MoveByTimeRange, MoveByTimeRangeParams>(reg,
        "MoveByTimeRange",
        {.name = "MoveByTimeRange",
         .description = "Move entities in a time range between same-type data objects",
         .category = "data_mutation",
         .supports_undo = true,
         .supported_data_types = {"LineData", "PointData", "MaskData", "DigitalEventSeries"}});
    // ...
}
```

#### Step 7.3 — Domain Library Registration Functions

Domain libraries define their own registration functions. The domain library links to
`Commands` (upward dependency) — `Commands` never links to the domain library:

```cpp
// src/TransformsV2/commands/register_transform_commands.hpp
namespace transforms {
void register_transform_commands();
}

// src/TransformsV2/commands/register_transform_commands.cpp
#include <Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.hpp>
#include "RunTransform.hpp"

namespace transforms {
void register_transform_commands() {
    commands::registerTypedCommand<RunTransformCommand, RunTransformParams>(
        commands::CommandRegistry::instance(),
        "RunTransform",
        {.name = "RunTransform",
         .description = "Execute a TransformsV2 pipeline on a data object",
         .category = "transform",
         .supports_undo = false});
}
}
```

The `RunTransform` command itself delegates to `ElementRegistry::instance()` at runtime —
it doesn't need to know about individual transforms at compile time. See
[Tier 2: Gateway Commands](../../inter_widget_communication_roadmap.md#ownership--registration-best-practices)
for the full gateway pattern design.

#### Step 7.4 — Central Dispatcher (Composition Root)

The dispatcher lives in the executable — the **composition root** that knows about all
concrete libraries. This is the only file that must be edited when a new library contributes
commands:

```cpp
// src/WhiskerToolbox/register_all_commands.hpp
void register_all_commands();

// src/WhiskerToolbox/register_all_commands.cpp
#include <Commands/Core/register_core_commands.hpp>
// future: #include <TransformsV2/commands/register_transform_commands.hpp>
// future: #include <MLCore/commands/register_mlcore_commands.hpp>

void register_all_commands() {
    commands::register_core_commands();
    // future: transforms::register_transform_commands();
    // future: mlcore::register_mlcore_commands();
}
```

Called once at startup:

```cpp
// main.cpp (or MainWindow constructor)
register_all_commands();  // before any command usage
```

**Failure modes:**

- **Forget to add the call to the dispatcher:** Registration function exists but is never
  called. The commands silently don't appear. However, unlike RAII, the function is a named
  exported symbol easily detectable by grep or static analysis. A CI check can verify all
  `register_*_commands()` functions are called somewhere.
- **Forget to add the `#include` but add the call:** Compiler error (undeclared function).
- **Add the `#include` and call, but the library doesn't define the function:** Linker error
  (undefined reference). **This is the critical advantage** — the most common mistake
  (misconfigured build) produces a loud failure instead of silent absence.

#### Step 7.5 — Dependency Flow

```
CommandRegistry (in libCommands.a)
       ↑ links to
  MLCore (in libMLCore.a)
       ↑ links to (normal, no --whole-archive)
  WhiskerToolbox executable
       │
       ├── links libCommands.a     (normal linking)
       ├── links libMLCore.a       (normal linking)
       └── compiles register_all_commands.cpp
               calls register_core_commands()    → pulls in Commands registrations
               calls register_mlcore_commands()  → pulls in MLCore registrations
```

Because the dispatcher explicitly calls each registration function, the linker follows the
call graph and pulls in the referenced command classes. No `--whole-archive` needed.

#### Step 7.6 — Test Registration

Tests register only the commands they need — no global dispatcher, no `--whole-archive`:

```cpp
// tests/Commands/test_setup.cpp
#include <Commands/Core/register_core_commands.hpp>

struct TestSetup {
    TestSetup() { commands::register_core_commands(); }
};
static TestSetup const setup;  // or call in a Catch2 listener
```

Tests for domain commands include only that domain's registration:

```cpp
// tests/MLCore/test_mlcore_commands.cpp
#include <MLCore/commands/register_mlcore_commands.hpp>
static struct { /* constructor calls register */} setup;
```

This is simpler than the `--whole-archive` approach, eliminates the cross-platform linker
flag conditional blocks, and gives tests precise control over which commands are available.

#### Step 7.7 — Validation Compatibility

The existing `validateSequence()` calls `isKnownCommandName()` for its structural check.
Since this delegates to `CommandRegistry::instance().isKnown()`, it automatically recognizes
domain-registered commands with no changes needed.

`getAvailableCommands()` and `getCommandInfo()` similarly delegate to the registry, so
AutoParamWidget-based UIs (Guided Pipeline Editor, future CommandSequenceWidget) automatically
discover domain commands.

### Migration Steps

- [ ] **Step 7.1:** Create `CommandRegistry` class with `registerCommand()`, `isKnown()`,
  `create()`, `availableCommands()`, `commandInfo()`. Add `registerTypedCommand<>` helper.
- [ ] **Step 7.2:** Create `register_core_commands()` in `src/Commands/Core/`, registering
  all 7 existing commands.
- [ ] **Step 7.3:** Remove the if-else chain and hardcoded `CommandInfo` vector from
  `CommandFactory.cpp`. Keep the free-function API as thin wrappers delegating to
  `CommandRegistry::instance()`.
- [ ] **Step 7.4:** Create `register_all_commands.cpp` in `src/WhiskerToolbox/`, calling
  `register_core_commands()`. Wire into `main.cpp` or `MainWindow` constructor.
- [ ] **Step 7.5:** Run all existing command tests — factory, validation, golden traces,
  recorder — to verify zero regressions. Update test setup to call
  `register_core_commands()` instead of relying on static initialization.
- [ ] **Step 7.6:** Add a Catch2 test that calls `CommandRegistry::instance().availableCommands()`
  and asserts all expected commands are present.
- [ ] **Step 7.7:** Remove any `--whole-archive` flags that were previously needed for
  Commands (if any). Verify normal linking suffices.

### Exit Criteria

1. `CommandFactory.cpp` contains zero if-else branches and zero `#include` of individual
   command headers.
2. All existing tests pass without modification (beyond registration setup changes).
3. A new command can be added by: (a) creating the command class, (b) adding a
   `registerTypedCommand<>` call in the library's registration function, and (c) if it's
   a new library, adding one line to `register_all_commands.cpp`. No `--whole-archive`
   flags needed.
4. `getAvailableCommands()` reports all registered commands including any from domain
   libraries.
5. No `--whole-archive` / `-force_load` / `/WHOLEARCHIVE` flags in any CMakeLists.txt
   for Commands.

### Comparison with Existing Registries

The explicit dispatcher approach diverges from the RAII pattern used by TransformsV2 and
DataSynthesizer. This is a deliberate choice — the `--whole-archive` drawbacks are
significant enough for Commands (which will span many domain libraries) to justify a
different registration strategy.

| Aspect | TransformsV2 (RAII) | DataSynthesizer (RAII) | Commands (Explicit Dispatcher) |
|--------|---------------------|----------------------|-------------------------------|
| Registration mechanism | `RegisterTransform<>` static object | `RegisterGenerator<>` static object | `register_*_commands()` function call |
| Central file touch | CMakeLists.txt (`--whole-archive`) | CMakeLists.txt (`--whole-archive`) | `register_all_commands.cpp` (one line) |
| `--whole-archive` | Required | Required | **Not needed** |
| Failure on misconfiguration | Silent (missing registrations) | Silent (missing registrations) | **Linker error** (undefined reference) |
| Binary size overhead | All `.o` files in archive forced in | All `.o` files in archive forced in | Only referenced code linked |
| Platform-specific flags | 3 variants (Linux/macOS/MSVC) | 3 variants (Linux/macOS/MSVC) | None |
| Test setup | `--whole-archive` in test CMakeLists | `--whole-archive` in test CMakeLists | Single function call per library |
| Shared library compatible | Singleton identity risk | Singleton identity risk | Works with both static and shared |

**Future consideration:** If the `--whole-archive` drawbacks become painful for TransformsV2
or DataSynthesizer as they grow, the same explicit dispatcher pattern can be applied to those
registries. The `CommandRegistry` singleton design (Step 7.1) is identical in both approaches —
only the registration mechanism differs.

---

## Relationship to State Management Roadmap

This roadmap replaces Phases 4–6 of the
[State Management Roadmap](../../STATE_MANAGEMENT_ROADMAP.md) with a more comprehensive
command-first architecture. The key differences:

| State Mgmt Roadmap (old) | Command Architecture (this doc) |
|---|---|
| Phase 4: Action Journal records ad-hoc action types | Phase 4 here: records `CommandDescriptor`s — standardized, replayable |
| Phase 5: `Command` interface with `execute()`/`undo()` | Phases 1+5 here: `ICommand` base with factory, `CommandStack` layered on top |
| Phase 6: `SessionReplayer` replays workspace provenance | Phase 6 here: CLI executor replays `CommandSequenceDescriptor` |
| No concept of triage or batch operations | Triage Session (Phase 2) demonstrates the architecture |

Ideas **carried forward** from the State Management Roadmap:

- **Action Journal** (`ActionEntry`, append-only log, JSONL format, query/filter) — Phase 4
- **CommandStack** (undo/redo, clean state, merge) — Phase 5
- **Memento-based EditorState undo** (`WidgetStateChangeCommand`) — Phase 5
- **Command merging** for sequential similar operations — Phase 5 (`ICommand::mergeWith()`)
- **ConfigExportToolbar** ("Copy as JSON", "Save Configuration...") — Phase 6
- **Session replay / batch processing** — Phase 6
- **Incremental adoption** (not every operation needs to be a command immediately) — design principle

## See Also

- [Command Architecture Design Document](index.qmd) — full architecture, types, and code examples
- [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd) — widget audit tables and new command designs
- [Data Manager](../data_manager.qmd) — core data management documentation
